question.english: How may eating habits change in the coming decades?
question.chinese: 未來幾十年，飲食習慣可能會怎樣改變？

source_lines.text: Topic: How may eating habits change in the coming decades?
未來幾十年，飲食習慣可能會怎樣改變？

components.english: I think eating habits will probably become more convenient, flexible, and technology-driven.
components.chinese: 我認為未來的飲食習慣很可能會變得更方便、更有彈性，也更受科技影響。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think eating habits will probably become more convenient, flexible,
and technology-driven.
[我認為未來的飲食習慣很可能會變得更方便、更有彈性，也更受科技影響。]

components.english: One reason is that people’s lives are already getting busier, so food is likely to fit around their schedule rather than the other way round. As delivery apps, smart devices, and nutrition-tracking tools become more common, people may rely more on ready-made meals, meal kits, or personalised food plans. In other words, eating may become less about sitting down for three fixed meals and more about finding something quick and suitable while on the go.
components.chinese: 其中一個原因是，人們的生活節奏已經越來越忙，所以食物很可能會變成配合人的時間表，而不是相反。隨著外賣應用程式、智能裝置，以及營養追蹤工具變得更普遍，人們可能會更依賴即食餐、配餐包，或者個人化飲食計劃。換句話說，進食可能會愈來愈少是固定坐下來吃三餐，而是更多變成在忙碌之中找些快捷又適合自己的食物。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: One reason is that people’s lives are already getting busier, so
food is likely to fit around their schedule rather than the other way round. As
delivery apps, smart devices, and nutrition-tracking tools become more
common, people may rely more on ready-made meals, meal kits, or personalised
food plans. In other words, eating may become less about sitting down for three
fixed meals and more about finding something quick and suitable while on the
go.
[其中一個原因是，人們的生活節奏已經越來越忙，所以食物很可能會變成配合人的時間表
，而不是相反。隨著外賣應用程式、智能裝置，以及營養追蹤工具變得更普遍，人們可能會
更依賴即食餐、配餐包，或者個人化飲食計劃。換句話說，進食可能會愈來愈少是固定坐下
來吃三餐，而是更多變成在忙碌之中找些快捷又適合自己的食物。]

components.english: For example, someone working in an office twenty years from now might order lunch through an app that matches their calorie needs, pick up a high-protein breakfast on the way to work, and cook a quick meal kit at night instead of preparing everything from scratch.
components.chinese: 例如，二十年後一位上班族可能會透過應用程式訂一份符合自己熱量需求的午餐、在上班路上買一份高蛋白早餐，晚上再煮一份快速配餐包，而不是從頭開始準備整頓飯。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, someone working in an office twenty years from now
might order lunch through an app that matches their calorie needs, pick up a
high-protein breakfast on the way to work, and cook a quick meal kit at night
instead of preparing everything from scratch.
[例如，二十年後一位上班族可能會透過應用程式訂一份符合自己熱量需求的午餐、在上班
路上買一份高蛋白早餐，晚上再煮一份快速配餐包，而不是從頭開始準備整頓飯。]

components.english: So, one likely change is that eating will become quicker and more personalised, with technology playing a bigger role in what, when, and how people eat.
components.chinese: 所以，其中一個很可能出現的改變，就是飲食會變得更快速、更個人化，而科技也會在吃甚麼、何時吃，以及怎樣吃這些事情上扮演更大的角色。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one likely change is that eating will become quicker and more
personalised, with technology playing a bigger role in what, when, and how
people eat.
[所以，其中一個很可能出現的改變，就是飲食會變得更快速、更個人化，而科技也會在吃
甚麼、何時吃，以及怎樣吃這些事情上扮演更大的角色。]

components.english: At the same time, I also think people may become more health-conscious and more aware of what their food does to both their body and the planet.
components.chinese: 同時，我也認為人們會變得更重視健康，也更在意食物對身體以及地球帶來甚麼影響。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: At the same time, I also think people may become more
health-conscious and more aware of what their food does to both their body and
the planet.
[同時，我也認為人們會變得更重視健康，也更在意食物對身體以及地球帶來甚麼影響。]

components.english: As awareness of nutrition, food quality, and environmental issues grows, many people may cut back on sugary drinks, ultra-processed food, or excessive meat, and choose fresher or more plant-based options instead. That does not mean everyone will suddenly eat perfectly, but the tide may well turn. People may start to see food not just as fuel, but as part of long-term wellbeing and even personal values.
components.chinese: 隨著人們對營養、食物品質，以及環境議題的認識加深，很多人可能會減少含糖飲品、高度加工食品，或者過量吃肉，轉而選擇更新鮮、更多植物性的食物。這並不代表每個人都會突然吃得完美無缺，但整體方向很可能會慢慢轉變。人們可能會開始把食物看成不只是填飽肚子的燃料，而是和長遠健康，甚至個人價值觀都有關的東西。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: As awareness of nutrition, food quality, and environmental
issues grows, many people may cut back on sugary drinks, ultra-processed food,
or excessive meat, and choose fresher or more plant-based options instead. That
does not mean everyone will suddenly eat perfectly, but the tide may well turn.
People may start to see food not just as fuel, but as part of long-term wellbeing
and even personal values.
[隨著人們對營養、食物品質，以及環境議題的認識加深，很多人可能會減少含糖飲品、高
度加工食品，或者過量吃肉，轉而選擇更新鮮、更多植物性的食物。這並不代表每個人都會
突然吃得完美無缺，但整體方向很可能會慢慢轉變。人們可能會開始把食物看成不只是填
飽肚子的燃料，而是和長遠健康，甚至個人價值觀都有關的東西。]

components.english: For instance, a family in the future might have a few meat-free days each week, read labels more carefully, and choose food that is lower in sugar or produced more responsibly. What ends up on the plate may reflect not only taste, but also health goals and environmental concerns.
components.chinese: 例如，未來一個家庭可能每星期會有幾天不吃肉、更仔細看食物標籤，並選擇糖分較低或生產方式更負責任的食物。最後放在餐桌上的東西，反映的可能不只是口味，還有健康目標和對環境的考量。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a family in the future might have a few meat-free
days each week, read labels more carefully, and choose food that is lower in sugar
or produced more responsibly. What ends up on the plate may reflect not only
taste, but also health goals and environmental concerns.
[例如，未來一個家庭可能每星期會有幾天不吃肉、更仔細看食物標籤，並選擇糖分較低或
生產方式更負責任的食物。最後放在餐桌上的東西，反映的可能不只是口味，還有健康目標
和對環境的考量。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say eating habits in the coming decades will probably become both faster and more thoughtful: faster in the way people eat, but more thoughtful in the way they choose.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說未來幾十年的飲食習慣很可能會同時變得更快，也更有意識：人們吃的方式會更快，但選擇食物時卻可能更用心。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say eating habits in the coming decades will
probably become both faster and more thoughtful: faster in the way people eat,
but more thoughtful in the way they choose.
[總結來說，我會說未來幾十年的飲食習慣很可能會同時變得更快，也更有意識：人們吃的
方式會更快，但選擇食物時卻可能更用心。]

question.english: Do you think our diet is important?
question.chinese: 你認為我們的飲食重要嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think our diet is important?
主題：你認為我們的飲食重要嗎？

components.english: Yes, absolutely, I think our diet is very important, because what we eat shapes not only our physical health, but also our energy levels, mood, and overall quality of life.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對重要。我認為我們的飲食非常重要，因為我們吃甚麼，不但會影響身體健康，還會影響精力、情緒，以及整體生活質素。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, absolutely, I think our diet is very important, because what we eat
shapes not only our physical health, but also our energy levels, mood, and overall
quality of life.
[是的，絕對重要。我認為我們的飲食非常重要，因為我們吃甚麼，不但會影響身體健康，還
會影響精力、情緒，以及整體生活質素。]

components.english: Food is not just something that fills the stomach; it is the fuel that keeps the body running. If people live on greasy takeaway food, sugary drinks, or highly processed snacks, they may feel sluggish, gain weight, or run into health problems sooner or later. By contrast, a balanced diet can help people stay energetic, think more clearly, and keep their body in better shape. In that sense, diet is a bit like the foundation of a house: if the base is weak, everything built on top of it becomes less stable.
components.chinese: 食物不只是拿來填飽肚子的東西；它其實是讓身體運作的燃料。如果一個人長期靠油膩外賣、含糖飲品，或者高度加工的小食過日子，他遲早可能會變得沒精神、體重上升，甚至出現健康問題。相反，均衡飲食能幫助人保持活力、思路更清晰，也讓身體狀態更穩定。從這個角度來說，飲食有點像一間房子的地基：如果底子不穩，上面的一切也很難真正穩固。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Food is not just something that fills the stomach; it is the fuel
that keeps the body running. If people live on greasy takeaway food, sugary
drinks, or highly processed snacks, they may feel sluggish, gain weight, or run
into health problems sooner or later. By contrast, a balanced diet can help people
stay energetic, think more clearly, and keep their body in better shape. In that
sense, diet is a bit like the foundation of a house: if the base is weak, everything
built on top of it becomes less stable.
[食物不只是拿來填飽肚子的東西；它其實是讓身體運作的燃料。如果一個人長期靠油膩外
賣、含糖飲品，或者高度加工的小食過日子，他遲早可能會變得沒精神、體重上升，甚至出
現健康問題。相反，均衡飲食能幫助人保持活力、思路更清晰，也讓身體狀態更穩定。從這
個角度來說，飲食有點像一間房子的地基：如果底子不穩，上面的一切也很難真正穩固。]

components.english: For example, if a student skips breakfast and eats fast food all the time, they may find it harder to concentrate in class and more difficult to stay alert throughout the day. On the other hand, if they eat regular meals with vegetables, protein, and enough water, they are much more likely to feel focused and productive.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個學生經常不吃早餐，又總是吃快餐，他在上課時可能就會比較難集中，也更難整天保持清醒。相反，如果他有規律地進食，而且吃到蔬菜、蛋白質，再加上足夠的水分，他通常就更容易保持專注，也更有學習效率。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a student skips breakfast and eats fast food all the
time, they may find it harder to concentrate in class and more difficult to stay
alert throughout the day. On the other hand, if they eat regular meals with
vegetables, protein, and enough water, they are much more likely to feel focused
and productive.
[例如，如果一個學生經常不吃早餐，又總是吃快餐，他在上課時可能就會比較難集中，也更
難整天保持清醒。相反，如果他有規律地進食，而且吃到蔬菜、蛋白質，再加上足夠的水分，
他通常就更容易保持專注，也更有學習效率。]

components.english: So yes, I would definitely say diet matters a great deal, because it affects how people feel, function, and live on a day-to-day basis.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我肯定會說飲食非常重要，因為它會直接影響一個人每天的感受、狀態，以及生活方式。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would definitely say diet matters a great deal, because it
affects how people feel, function, and live on a day-to-day basis.
[所以，是的，我肯定會說飲食非常重要，因為它會直接影響一個人每天的感受、狀態，以及
生活方式。]

components.english: That said, I do not think having an important diet means people need to eat perfectly all the time or turn every meal into a source of stress.
components.chinese: 不過，我不認為飲食重要就代表人一定要每一餐都吃得完美，或者把吃東西這件事變成一種壓力來源。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think having an important diet means people need
to eat perfectly all the time or turn every meal into a source of stress.
[不過，我不認為飲食重要就代表人一定要每一餐都吃得完美，或者把吃東西這件事變成一
種壓力來源。]

components.english: To be honest, if people become too strict or obsessive about food, that can backfire. Healthy eating should be sustainable, not punishing. In real life, balance matters more than perfection. A good diet is not about never touching dessert or cutting out every comfort food under the sun; rather, it is about building sensible habits most of the time. After all, eating well should support life, not take the joy out of it.
components.chinese: 老實說，如果一個人對食物變得過分嚴格，甚至近乎執著，那反而可能適得其反。健康飲食應該是可持續的，而不是帶有懲罰意味的。在現實生活中，平衡往往比完美更重要。好的飲食並不是指永遠不能吃甜品，或者把所有令人快樂的食物都一刀切掉；更準確地說，它是指在大多數時候建立合理而穩定的飲食習慣。畢竟，吃得健康應該是為了支持生活，而不是把生活的樂趣都拿走。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, if people become too strict or obsessive about
food, that can backfire. Healthy eating should be sustainable, not punishing. In
real life, balance matters more than perfection. A good diet is not about never

生活的樂趣都拿走。]

components.english: For instance, someone who usually eats nutritious home-cooked meals can still enjoy a burger or a slice of cake now and then without ruining their health. It is the overall pattern that counts, not one meal here and there.
components.chinese: 例如，一個平時主要吃有營養家常菜的人，偶爾吃一個漢堡包，或者一塊蛋糕，其實並不會因此毀掉健康。真正重要的是整體模式，而不是某一餐半餐。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, someone who usually eats nutritious home-cooked
meals can still enjoy a burger or a slice of cake now and then without ruining
their health. It is the overall pattern that counts, not one meal here and there.
[例如，一個平時主要吃有營養家常菜的人，偶爾吃一個漢堡包，或者一塊蛋糕，其實並不會
因此毀掉健康。真正重要的是整體模式，而不是某一餐半餐。]

components.english: Overall, I think diet is genuinely important, because it acts like fuel for both the body and daily life, and it can shape our health, energy, and overall condition in the long run. However, the best approach is not to chase extremes, but to aim for balance, consistency, and sustainability.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我認為飲食真的很重要，因為它就像身體和日常生活的燃料一樣，會長遠影響我們的健康、精力和整體狀態。不過，最理想的做法不是追求極端，而是追求平衡、穩定和可持續。

overall condition in the long run. However, the best approach is not to chase

可持續。]
Topic: What is a balanced diet?
甚麼是均衡飲食？

components.english: A balanced diet, in my view, is one that gives the body the right mix of nutrients in the right amounts, rather than too much of one thing and too little of another.
components.chinese: 在我看來，均衡飲食就是讓身體以適當分量攝取各種所需營養，而不是某一類吃得太多，某一類又吃得太少。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: A balanced diet, in my view, is one that gives the body the right mix of
nutrients in the right amounts, rather than too much of one thing and too little
of another.
[在我看來，均衡飲食就是讓身體以適當分量攝取各種所需營養，而不是某一類吃得太多，
某一類又吃得太少。]

components.english: The key point is balance. A healthy diet is not built on extremes, fad trends, or the idea that one “superfood” can work miracles. Instead, it usually includes a sensible mix of vegetables, fruit, whole grains, protein, healthy fats, and enough water. In other words, a balanced diet is about giving the body a full toolbox, not asking it to run on fumes. If people eat in a varied and moderate way, they are much more likely to stay healthy and energetic in the long run.
components.chinese: 重點其實就在於「平衡」二字。健康飲食並不是建立在極端做法、流行飲食法，或者相信某一種「超級食物」能包辦一切的想法之上。相反，它通常包括合理比例的蔬菜、水果、全穀類、蛋白質、健康脂肪，以及足夠的水分。換句話說，均衡飲食就是替身體提供一整套完整工具，而不是讓它在快要沒油的狀態下勉強運作。如果人們能以多樣而適量的方式進食，長遠來說就更有可能保持健康和活力。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The key point is balance. A healthy diet is not built on extremes,
fad trends, or the idea that one “superfood” can work miracles. Instead, it
usually includes a sensible mix of vegetables, fruit, whole grains, protein, healthy
fats, and enough water. In other words, a balanced diet is about giving the body
a full toolbox, not asking it to run on fumes. If people eat in a varied and
moderate way, they are much more likely to stay healthy and energetic in the
long run.
[重點其實就在於「平衡」二字。健康飲食並不是建立在極端做法、流行飲食法，或者相信某
一種「超級食物」能包辦一切的想法之上。相反，它通常包括合理比例的蔬菜、水果、全穀
類、蛋白質、健康脂肪，以及足夠的水分。換句話說，均衡飲食就是替身體提供一整套完整
工具，而不是讓它在快要沒油的狀態下勉強運作。如果人們能以多樣而適量的方式進食，長
遠來說就更有可能保持健康和活力。]

components.english: For example, a balanced meal might include rice or pasta for energy, fish, eggs, or beans for protein, and a good portion of vegetables for vitamins and fibre. A piece of fruit or some yoghurt could also round the meal out nicely. It does not have to be fancy; it just has to make nutritional sense.
components.chinese: 例如，一餐均衡的飯菜可能會包括米飯或意粉來提供能量，魚、雞蛋或豆類來提供蛋白質，再加上一份足夠的蔬菜來提供維他命和纖維。之後再配一份水果或乳酪，也能令整餐更完整。它不一定要很花巧；重點是整體營養要說得通。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a balanced meal might include rice or pasta for energy,
fish, eggs, or beans for protein, and a good portion of vegetables for vitamins
and fibre. A piece of fruit or some yoghurt could also round the meal out nicely.
It does not have to be fancy; it just has to make nutritional sense.
[例如，一餐均衡的飯菜可能會包括米飯或意粉來提供能量，魚、雞蛋或豆類來提供蛋白質

整。它不一定要很花巧；重點是整體營養要說得通。]

components.english: So, to me, a balanced diet is basically about variety, moderation, and nutritional common sense.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，均衡飲食其實就是建立在多樣性、適量，以及基本營養常識之上的。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, to me, a balanced diet is basically about variety, moderation,
and nutritional common sense.
[所以，在我看來，均衡飲食其實就是建立在多樣性、適量，以及基本營養常識之上的。]

components.english: That said, I do not think a balanced diet means eating perfectly all the time, because that is neither realistic nor necessary.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為均衡飲食代表每一餐都要吃得完美無缺，因為那既不現實，也沒有必要。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think a balanced diet means eating perfectly all the
time, because that is neither realistic nor necessary.
[不過，我並不認為均衡飲食代表每一餐都要吃得完美無缺，因為那既不現實，也沒有必

components.english: To be honest, if people become too rigid about food, healthy eating can start to feel like a prison rather than a support system. A balanced diet should leave room for enjoyment as well as nutrition. It is perfectly possible to eat well most of the time and still have dessert, snacks, or the occasional indulgent meal without the sky falling in. In that sense, balance is not about being flawless; it is about finding a rhythm the body can thrive on and the person can actually stick to.
components.chinese: 老實說，如果一個人對飲食要求得過於死板，那麼健康飲食很容易由一種支持，變成一個牢籠。均衡飲食應該同時為營養和享受都留出空間。一個人完全可以大部分時間都吃得健康，同時仍然偶爾吃甜品、零食，或者放縱一下，而不至於天塌下來。從這個角度來說，平衡並不是要求自己毫無瑕疵，而是找到一種既能讓身體受益、又能真正長期堅持下去的節奏。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, if people become too rigid about food, healthy

並不是要求自己毫無瑕疵，而是找到一種既能讓身體受益、又能真正長期堅持下去的節

components.english: For instance, someone who eats nourishing meals during the week but also enjoys pizza with friends or a slice of cake now and then can still have a balanced diet overall. What matters is the bigger picture, not just one plate or one day.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人如果平日大多數時間都吃得有營養，但偶爾也會和朋友一起吃薄餅，或者吃一塊蛋糕，他整體上仍然可以有均衡飲食。真正重要的是整體圖畫，而不是某一碟食物或某一天。

components.english: Overall, I’d say a balanced diet is one that nourishes the body without turning food into a burden. It should be healthy, sustainable, and realistic enough to work in real life, not just on paper.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說均衡飲食就是一種既能滋養身體，又不會把吃東西變成負擔的方式。它應該是健康的、可持續的，而且足夠現實，能在真正的生活裡行得通，而不只是紙上談兵。

question.english: How might eating habits change in the coming decades?
question.chinese: 未來幾十年，人們的飲食習慣可能會怎樣改變？

source_lines.text: Topic: How might eating habits change in the coming decades?
未來幾十年，人們的飲食習慣可能會怎樣改變？

components.english: I think eating habits will probably become even more convenience-driven and more personalised than they are now.
components.chinese: 我認為，未來的飲食習慣很可能會變得比現在更加講求方便，也更加個人化。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think eating habits will probably become even more
convenience-driven and more personalised than they are now.
[我認為，未來的飲食習慣很可能會變得比現在更加講求方便，也更加個人化。]

components.english: The main reason is that modern life is already fast-paced, and that trend is unlikely to slow down. As people get busier, they will probably rely more on ready-made meals, food delivery, smart kitchen technology, and diets tailored to their own needs. In other words, people may eat with one eye on convenience and the other on efficiency. Food will not just be about taste; it will also be about speed, flexibility, and fitting into a packed schedule.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，現代生活本來就已經節奏很快，而這個趨勢大概也不會慢下來。隨著人們變得越來越忙，他們很可能會更依賴即食餐、外賣平台、智能廚房科技，以及按個人需要而設計的飲食方式。換句話說，人們未來吃東西時，可能一方面看重方便，另一方面也看重效率。食物不再只是關乎味道，也會愈來愈關乎速度、彈性，以及能否塞進忙碌的生活節奏裡。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that modern life is already fast-paced, and
that trend is unlikely to slow down. As people get busier, they will probably rely
more on ready-made meals, food delivery, smart kitchen technology, and diets
tailored to their own needs. In other words, people may eat with one eye on
convenience and the other on efficiency. Food will not just be about taste; it will
also be about speed, flexibility, and fitting into a packed schedule.
[主要原因是，現代生活本來就已經節奏很快，而這個趨勢大概也不會慢下來。隨著人們變
得越來越忙，他們很可能會更依賴即食餐、外賣平台、智能廚房科技，以及按個人需要而設
計的飲食方式。換句話說，人們未來吃東西時，可能一方面看重方便，另一方面也看重效
率。食物不再只是關乎味道，也會愈來愈關乎速度、彈性，以及能否塞進忙碌的生活節奏

components.english: For example, it would not surprise me if more people started using apps to plan meals, count nutrients, or order food that matches their health goals. A busy office worker might have breakfast on the go, a customised lunch delivered to the office, and a quick but balanced dinner prepared with the help of smart appliances at home.
components.chinese: 例如，如果將來有更多人用應用程式來規劃三餐、計算營養，或者訂購符合自己健康目標的食物，我一點也不會覺得奇怪。一位忙碌的上班族可能會邊走邊吃早餐，中午在辦公室收到度身訂造的午餐，晚上則在家借助智能家電，快速準備一頓相對均衡的晚餐。

components.english: So, one likely change is that eating will become more tailored, more efficient, and more tightly woven into people’s daily routines.
components.chinese: 所以，一個很可能出現的轉變就是：人們的飲食會變得更度身訂造、更有效率，也更緊密地嵌進日常生活之中。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one likely change is that eating will become more tailored,
more efficient, and more tightly woven into people’s daily routines.
[所以，一個很可能出現的轉變就是：人們的飲食會變得更度身訂造、更有效率，也更緊密
地嵌進日常生活之中。]

components.english: That said, I also think people may become more health-conscious and environmentally aware, so the future plate may look greener and more deliberate than it does today.
components.chinese: 不過，我也認為人們可能會變得更重視健康，也更有環保意識，所以未來餐桌上的食物，可能會比今天看起來更偏向綠色、更有意識地被選擇。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I also think people may become more health-conscious and
environmentally aware, so the future plate may look greener and more
deliberate than it does today.
[不過，我也認為人們可能會變得更重視健康，也更有環保意識，所以未來餐桌上的食物，
可能會比今天看起來更偏向綠色、更有意識地被選擇。]

components.english: In recent years, more people have started paying attention to sugar, oil, processed food, and where their food actually comes from. I think that tendency will grow stronger. People may eat less meat, choose more plant-based options, and think more seriously about sustainability, food waste, and long-term health. At the same time, though, it may be a mixed picture. While some people will eat more wisely, others may lean even more heavily on ultra-convenient food. So the future could be healthier in some ways, but not automatically healthier across the board.
components.chinese: 近年來，越來越多人開始留意糖分、油分、加工食品，以及食物究竟來自哪裡。我認為這種傾向將來會更明顯。人們可能會少吃肉、多選植物性食物，也會更認真地思考可持續發展、食物浪費，以及長遠健康。不過，同一時間，情況也可能是好壞並存的。一方面，有些人會吃得更有智慧；另一方面，也有些人可能會更加依賴極度方便的即食食品。所以，未來的飲食在某些方面可能會更健康，但未必會全面地自動變得更健康。

[近年來，越來越多人開始留意糖分、油分、加工食品，以及食物究竟來自哪裡。我認為這種
傾向將來會更明顯。人們可能會少吃肉、多選植物性食物，也會更認真地思考可持續發展、
食物浪費，以及長遠健康。不過，同一時間，情況也可能是好壞並存的。一方面，有些人會吃
得更有智慧；另一方面，也有些人可能會更加依賴極度方便的即食食品。所以，未來的飲食
在某些方面可能會更健康，但未必會全面地自動變得更健康。]

components.english: For instance, it may become normal for families to buy plant-based meat, low-sugar drinks, or locally sourced food as part of everyday life. On the other hand, younger people who are short on time may still fall back on late-night delivery, snack foods, and quick fixes when life gets hectic.
components.chinese: 例如，將來家庭把植物肉、低糖飲品，或者本地食材當成日常選擇，可能會變得很普通。另一方面，那些時間緊迫的年輕人，在生活忙得喘不過氣時，仍然可能會回到宵夜外賣、零食，以及各種快速解決方案之上。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, it may become normal for families to buy
plant-based meat, low-sugar drinks, or locally sourced food as part of everyday
life. On the other hand, younger people who are short on time may still fall back
on late-night delivery, snack foods, and quick fixes when life gets hectic.
[例如，將來家庭把植物肉、低糖飲品，或者本地食材當成日常選擇，可能會變得很普通。另
一方面，那些時間緊迫的年輕人，在生活忙得喘不過氣時，仍然可能會回到宵夜外賣、零食
，以及各種快速解決方案之上。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say eating habits in the coming decades will probably become more convenient, more personalised, and more health-aware, but also more divided. Some people will treat food almost like fuel with a function, whereas others will still eat for comfort, habit, or pleasure. So the habits may evolve, but the human side of eating will still be very much alive.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，未來幾十年的飲食習慣很可能會變得更方便、更個人化，也更重視健康，但同時也會更加分化。有些人可能會幾乎把食物當成功能性的燃料來看待，而另一些人仍然會為了安慰感、習慣，或者單純的享受而進食。所以，飲食習慣也許會演變，但進食這件事裡很「人」的一面，仍然會一直存在。

probably become more convenient, more personalised, and more health-aware,
but also more divided. Some people will treat food almost like fuel with a

habits may evolve, but the human side of eating will still be very much alive.

康，但同時也會更加分化。有些人可能會幾乎把食物當成功能性的燃料來看待，而另一些人
仍然會為了安慰感、習慣，或者單純的享受而進食。所以，飲食習慣也許會演變，但進食這
件事裡很「人」的一面，仍然會一直存在。]

question.english: How are education priorities today different from those in the past?
question.chinese: 今天的教育重點和過去有甚麼不同？

source_lines.text: Topic: How are education priorities today different from those in the past?
[主題：今天的教育重點和過去有甚麼不同？]

components.english: I’d say education today is generally broader and more future-focused than it used to be. In the past, the priority was often more about discipline, basic knowledge, and exam results.
components.chinese: 我會說，今天的教育整體上比過去更廣闊，也更着眼未來。過去的重點往往更集中在紀律、基礎知識，以及考試成績上。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I’d say education today is generally broader and more future-focused
than it used to be. In the past, the priority was often more about discipline,
basic knowledge, and exam results.
[我會說，今天的教育整體上比過去更廣闊，也更着眼未來。過去的重點往往更集中在紀
律、基礎知識，以及考試成績上。]

components.english: Years ago, education in many places was much more teacher-centred and textbook-driven. Students were often expected to sit still, follow instructions, memorise information, and get the right answers. That approach was not without value, because it built a solid foundation. However, the world has changed, and schools today are under more pressure to prepare students not just to pass tests, but to think critically, solve problems, communicate well, and adapt to change. In other words, the goalposts have moved. It is no longer only about filling students’ heads; it is also about helping them use their minds.
components.chinese: 很多年前，不少地方的教育都更以老師為中心，也更依賴課本。學生往往被期望安靜坐好、聽從指示、背誦內容，並且給出正確答案。這種做法並不是毫無價值，因為它的確能打下穩固基礎。不過，世界已經改變，今天的學校面對更大壓力，不只是要讓學生通過考試，還要培養他們的批判思維、解難能力、溝通技巧，以及適應變化的能力。換句話說，教育的目標已經移動了。現在不再只是把知識塞進學生腦袋裡；同時也要幫助他們學會如何運用自己的頭腦。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Years ago, education in many places was much more
teacher-centred and textbook-driven. Students were often expected to sit still,
follow instructions, memorise information, and get the right answers. That
approach was not without value, because it built a solid foundation. However,
the world has changed, and schools today are under more pressure to prepare
students not just to pass tests, but to think critically, solve problems,
communicate well, and adapt to change. In other words, the goalposts have
moved. It is no longer only about filling students’ heads; it is also about helping
them use their minds.
[很多年前，不少地方的教育都更以老師為中心，也更依賴課本。學生往往被期望安靜坐
好、聽從指示、背誦內容，並且給出正確答案。這種做法並不是毫無價值，因為它的確能打
下穩固基礎。不過，世界已經改變，今天的學校面對更大壓力，不只是要讓學生通過考試，
還要培養他們的批判思維、解難能力、溝通技巧，以及適應變化的能力。換句話說，教育的
目標已經移動了。現在不再只是把知識塞進學生腦袋裡；同時也要幫助他們學會如何運用
自己的頭腦。]

components.english: For example, in the past a lesson might have focused mainly on copying notes, memorising facts, and repeating model answers. These days, students are more likely to be asked to discuss ideas, work in groups, give presentations, or apply knowledge to real-life situations. So the classroom has become less like a one-way street and more like a space for interaction.
components.chinese: 例如，以前一堂課可能主要集中在抄筆記、背誦資料，以及重複標準答案。如今，學生更可能被要求討論想法、小組合作、作口頭匯報，或者把知識運用到現實情境之中。所以，課堂已經不那麼像一條單向道路，反而更像一個互動空間。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, in the past a lesson might have focused mainly on
copying notes, memorising facts, and repeating model answers. These days,
students are more likely to be asked to discuss ideas, work in groups, give
presentations, or apply knowledge to real-life situations. So the classroom has
become less like a one-way street and more like a space for interaction.
[例如，以前一堂課可能主要集中在抄筆記、背誦資料，以及重複標準答案。如今，學生更可
能被要求討論想法、小組合作、作口頭匯報，或者把知識運用到現實情境之中。所以，課堂
已經不那麼像一條單向道路，反而更像一個互動空間。]

components.english: So, the biggest difference, in my view, is that past education was often more about knowledge and obedience, whereas today’s education is expected to develop independence and flexibility as well.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，最大的分別在於：過去的教育往往更着重知識和服從，而今天的教育則同時被期望培養獨立性和靈活性。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, the biggest difference, in my view, is that past education was
often more about knowledge and obedience, whereas today’s education is
expected to develop independence and flexibility as well.
[所以，在我看來，最大的分別在於：過去的教育往往更着重知識和服從，而今天的教育則
同時被期望培養獨立性和靈活性。]

components.english: Another major difference is that schools today tend to care more about the whole child, not just the report card.
components.chinese: 另一個很大的分別是，今天的學校往往比過去更重視孩子的整體發展，而不只是成績表。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another major difference is that schools today tend to care more about
the whole child, not just the report card.
[另一個很大的分別是，今天的學校往往比過去更重視孩子的整體發展，而不只是成績表。]

components.english: In the past, academic success was often treated as the be-all and end-all, and subjects like maths, science, and language were usually put on a pedestal. Today, while grades still matter, there is generally more awareness that students also need emotional support, creativity, social skills, and even digital literacy. Schools are more likely to talk about mental health, confidence, collaboration, and personal growth. That is probably because modern life is more complex than it used to be. To put it simply, education today is not just about producing good students on paper; it is about preparing young people for a messy, fast-moving, real world.
components.chinese: 過去，學業成就往往被視為最重要、幾乎是一切，而數學、科學和語文這些科目通常會被放在特別高的位置。今天，雖然成績仍然重要，但人們普遍更意識到，學生同時也需要情緒支援、創意、社交能力，甚至數碼素養。學校現在更可能談到心理健康、自信心、合作能力，以及個人成長。這大概是因為現代生活比以前複雜得多。簡單來說，今天的教育不只是要在紙面上培養出「好學生」；它更是要為年輕人準備一個混亂、快速變動，而且真實的世界。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In the past, academic success was often treated as the be-all
and end-all, and subjects like maths, science, and language were usually put on a
pedestal. Today, while grades still matter, there is generally more awareness that
students also need emotional support, creativity, social skills, and even digital
literacy. Schools are more likely to talk about mental health, confidence,
collaboration, and personal growth. That is probably because modern life is
more complex than it used to be. To put it simply, education today is not just
about producing good students on paper; it is about preparing young people for
a messy, fast-moving, real world.
[過去，學業成就往往被視為最重要、幾乎是一切，而數學、科學和語文這些科目通常會被
放在特別高的位置。今天，雖然成績仍然重要，但人們普遍更意識到，學生同時也需要情緒
支援、創意、社交能力，甚至數碼素養。學校現在更可能談到心理健康、自信心、合作能力，
以及個人成長。這大概是因為現代生活比以前複雜得多。簡單來說，今天的教育不只是要在
紙面上培養出「好學生」；它更是要為年輕人準備一個混亂、快速變動，而且真實的世界。]

components.english: For instance, a school today may still care about exam scores, but it may also encourage students to join clubs, community service, leadership activities, or creative projects. Years ago, those things might have been seen as side dishes. Now, they are much more likely to be seen as part of the main course.
components.chinese: 例如，今天的學校當然仍然重視考試成績，但同時也可能鼓勵學生參加學會、社區服務、領袖活動，或者創意項目。放在以前，這些事情可能只是配菜；但現在，它們更有可能被視為主菜的一部分。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a school today may still care about exam scores, but
it may also encourage students to join clubs, community service, leadership
activities, or creative projects. Years ago, those things might have been seen as
side dishes. Now, they are much more likely to be seen as part of the main
course.
[例如，今天的學校當然仍然重視考試成績，但同時也可能鼓勵學生參加學會、社區服務、
領袖活動，或者創意項目。放在以前，這些事情可能只是配菜；但現在，它們更有可能被視
為主菜的一部分。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say education priorities today are less narrow than they were in the past. The old model was more about discipline, knowledge, and results, while the newer one tries to balance those things with creativity, wellbeing, and real-world readiness. Of course, the basics still matter, but the picture is now much bigger.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說今天的教育重點比過去沒有那麼狹窄。舊有模式更着重紀律、知識和成績，而新的模式則嘗試在這些元素之外，再加入創意、身心健康，以及面對現實世界的準備。當然，基礎依然重要，但整幅圖畫現在明顯大得多。

results, while the newer one tries to balance those things with creativity,
wellbeing, and real-world readiness. Of course, the basics still matter, but the
picture is now much bigger.
[總結來說，我會說今天的教育重點比過去沒有那麼狹窄。舊有模式更着重紀律、知識和成

備。當然，基礎依然重要，但整幅圖畫現在明顯大得多。]

question.english: What is your opinion on the way languages are taught in schools?
question.chinese: 你對學校教授語言的方式有甚麼看法？

source_lines.text: Topic: What is your opinion on the way languages are taught in schools?
你對學校教授語言的方式有甚麼看法？

components.english: To be honest, I think schools usually do a decent job of teaching the basics of a language, but the approach is often too exam-oriented and not communicative enough.
components.chinese: 老實說，我認為學校在教授語言基礎方面通常做得不錯，但整體方法往往太偏向考試，也不夠重視真實溝通。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: To be honest, I think schools usually do a decent job of teaching the
basics of a language, but the approach is often too exam-oriented and not
communicative enough.
[老實說，我認為學校在教授語言基礎方面通常做得不錯，但整體方法往往太偏向考試，也
不夠重視真實溝通。]

components.english: In many schools, students spend a lot of time memorising vocabulary, learning grammar rules, and practising fixed question types. That certainly helps them build a foundation, so I would not say the system is useless. However, language is not a museum piece to be admired behind glass; it is a living thing that people are supposed to use. If teaching focuses too heavily on getting the right answer on paper, students may know the language in theory but still clam up when it is time to speak.
components.chinese: 在很多學校裡，學生會花很多時間背生字、學文法規則，以及操練固定題型。這當然有助他們打好基礎，所以我不會說這套制度毫無價值。不過，語言並不是放在玻璃櫃裡供人欣賞的博物館展品；它是一種活生生、要被真正使用的東西。如果教學過分集中於在紙上寫出正確答案，學生就可能在理論上懂得那種語言，真正要開口時卻反而卡住。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In many schools, students spend a lot of time memorising
vocabulary, learning grammar rules, and practising fixed question types. That
certainly helps them build a foundation, so I would not say the system is useless.
However, language is not a museum piece to be admired behind glass; it is a
living thing that people are supposed to use. If teaching focuses too heavily on
getting the right answer on paper, students may know the language in theory
but still clam up when it is time to speak.
[在很多學校裡，學生會花很多時間背生字、學文法規則，以及操練固定題型。這當然有助
他們打好基礎，所以我不會說這套制度毫無價值。不過，語言並不是放在玻璃櫃裡供人欣
賞的博物館展品；它是一種活生生、要被真正使用的東西。如果教學過分集中於在紙上寫出
正確答案，學生就可能在理論上懂得那種語言，真正要開口時卻反而卡住。]

components.english: For example, some students can fill in the blanks perfectly or recite a long list of grammar points, but they still struggle to order a meal, ask for directions, or keep a simple conversation going. In that case, the language has stayed on the page instead of making the leap into real life.
components.chinese: 例如，有些學生可以非常準確地完成填充題，或者背出一長串文法重點，但他們仍然可能不懂得如何點餐、如何問路，或者如何把一段簡單對話自然地說下去。這種情況下，語言其實仍然停留在紙上，未真正跨進現實生活。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, some students can fill in the blanks perfectly or recite a
long list of grammar points, but they still struggle to order a meal, ask for
directions, or keep a simple conversation going. In that case, the language has
stayed on the page instead of making the leap into real life.
[例如，有些學生可以非常準確地完成填充題，或者背出一長串文法重點，但他們仍然可能
不懂得如何點餐、如何問路，或者如何把一段簡單對話自然地說下去。這種情況下，語言其
實仍然停留在紙上，未真正跨進現實生活。]

components.english: So, overall, my view is that school language teaching often builds knowledge quite well, but it does not always build confidence or fluency in the same way.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我的看法是，學校的語言教學往往能夠不錯地建立知識，但未必同樣能建立自信和流利度。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, overall, my view is that school language teaching often builds
knowledge quite well, but it does not always build confidence or fluency in the
same way.
[所以，整體來說，我的看法是，學校的語言教學往往能夠不錯地建立知識，但未必同樣能
建立自信和流利度。]

components.english: That said, I think language classes could become far more effective if schools taught language as a tool for connection rather than just a subject to pass.
components.chinese: 不過，我也認為，如果學校把語言視為一種用來連結他人的工具，而不只是要考及格的一個科目，那麼語言課其實可以變得有效得多。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I think language classes could become far more effective if
schools taught language as a tool for connection rather than just a subject to
pass.
[不過，我也認為，如果學校把語言視為一種用來連結他人的工具，而不只是要考及格的一
個科目，那麼語言課其實可以變得有效得多。]

components.english: I believe students learn languages best when they are encouraged to use them in meaningful, low-pressure ways, such as discussions, role-plays, presentations, storytelling, or even casual classroom interaction. After all, people do not learn to swim by reading about water. In the same way, they do not become confident speakers by living inside textbooks alone. If students are given more chances to speak, make mistakes, laugh a little, and try again, the language starts to feel less like a wall and more like a bridge.
components.chinese: 我相信，當學生被鼓勵以有意義而且壓力較低的方式去使用語言時，例如討論、角色扮演、簡報、說故事，甚至只是課堂上的自然互動，他們往往學得最好。畢竟，人不可能靠閱讀關於水的知識就學會游泳。同樣地，學生也不可能只活在課本裡，就自然變成有自信的說話者。如果他們有更多機會去開口、犯錯、笑一笑，再重新來過，那種語言就會慢慢不再像一面牆，而更像一座橋。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: I believe students learn languages best when they are
encouraged to use them in meaningful, low-pressure ways, such as discussions,
role-plays, presentations, storytelling, or even casual classroom interaction.
After all, people do not learn to swim by reading about water. In the same way,
they do not become confident speakers by living inside textbooks alone. If
students are given more chances to speak, make mistakes, laugh a little, and try
again, the language starts to feel less like a wall and more like a bridge.
[我相信，當學生被鼓勵以有意義而且壓力較低的方式去使用語言時，例如討論、角色扮
演、簡報、說故事，甚至只是課堂上的自然互動，他們往往學得最好。畢竟，人不可能靠閱讀
關於水的知識就學會游泳。同樣地，學生也不可能只活在課本裡，就自然變成有自信的說話
者。如果他們有更多機會去開口、犯錯、笑一笑，再重新來過，那種語言就會慢慢不再像一
面牆，而更像一座橋。]

components.english: For instance, if a teacher asks students to discuss a real topic, act out a situation at an airport, or respond to a short video, the lesson immediately feels more alive. Students may not speak perfectly at first, but they are far more likely to engage with the language instead of just tiptoeing around it.
components.chinese: 例如，如果老師要求學生討論一個真實話題、模擬機場情境，或者回應一段短片內容，整堂課立刻就會變得更有生命力。學生一開始說得未必完美，但他們更有可能真正投入那種語言，而不是只小心翼翼地繞著它走。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if a teacher asks students to discuss a real topic, act
out a situation at an airport, or respond to a short video, the lesson immediately
feels more alive. Students may not speak perfectly at first, but they are far more
likely to engage with the language instead of just tiptoeing around it.
[例如，如果老師要求學生討論一個真實話題、模擬機場情境，或者回應一段短片內容，整
堂課立刻就會變得更有生命力。學生一開始說得未必完美，但他們更有可能真正投入那種
語言，而不是只小心翼翼地繞著它走。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the way languages are taught in schools is useful up to a point, but it often needs more life, more interaction, and more room for real communication. In my opinion, the best language teaching does not just help students pass exams; it helps them find their voice.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，學校教授語言的方法在某個程度上是有用的，但它往往仍然需要更多生命力、更多互動，以及更多真實溝通的空間。在我看來，最好的語言教學不只是幫助學生考試過關；它更是幫助他們找到自己的聲音。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the way languages are taught in schools is useful
up to a point, but it often needs more life, more interaction, and more room for
real communication. In my opinion, the best language teaching does not just
help students pass exams; it helps them find their voice.
[總結來說，我會說，學校教授語言的方法在某個程度上是有用的，但它往往仍然需要更多
生命力、更多互動，以及更多真實溝通的空間。在我看來，最好的語言教學不只是幫助學生
考試過關；它更是幫助他們找到自己的聲音。]

question.english: How can the type of school you go to affect career success?
question.chinese: 你就讀的學校類型可以怎樣影響事業成功？

source_lines.text: Topic: How can the type of school you go to affect career success?
你就讀的學校類型可以怎樣影響事業成功？

components.english: I think the type of school a person goes to can affect career success quite a lot, mainly because it shapes the opportunities, support, and networks they are exposed to early on.
components.chinese: 我認為，一個人就讀的學校類型的確可以相當大程度地影響事業成功，主要因為它會影響一個人在早期接觸到甚麼樣的機會、支持，以及人脈。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the type of school a person goes to can affect career success quite
a lot, mainly because it shapes the opportunities, support, and networks they
are exposed to early on.
[我認為，一個人就讀的學校類型的確可以相當大程度地影響事業成功，主要因為它會影響
一個人在早期接觸到甚麼樣的機會、支持，以及人脈。]

components.english: Some schools simply have more to offer. They may have better resources, stronger teachers, more career guidance, and a culture that pushes students to aim higher. As a result, students there are often better placed to build confidence, develop useful skills, and gain experiences that look valuable later on. In some cases, the school’s reputation itself can also open doors, especially at the beginning of a person’s career.
components.chinese: 有些學校的確能提供更多東西。它們可能擁有更好的資源、更強的教師、更完整的升學或就業指導，以及一種鼓勵學生追求更高目標的氛圍。因此，那裡的學生通常會更有條件建立自信、培養實用能力，並累積一些日後看起來很有價值的經驗。在某些情況下，學校本身的名聲也能打開一些機會之門，特別是在一個人職涯剛開始的時候。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Some schools simply have more to offer. They may have better
resources, stronger teachers, more career guidance, and a culture that pushes
students to aim higher. As a result, students there are often better placed to
build confidence, develop useful skills, and gain experiences that look valuable
later on. In some cases, the school’s reputation itself can also open doors,
especially at the beginning of a person’s career.
[有些學校的確能提供更多東西。它們可能擁有更好的資源、更強的教師、更完整的升學或
就業指導，以及一種鼓勵學生追求更高目標的氛圍。因此，那裡的學生通常會更有條件建立
自信、培養實用能力，並累積一些日後看起來很有價值的經驗。在某些情況下，學校本身的
名聲也能打開一些機會之門，特別是在一個人職涯剛開始的時候。]

components.english: For example, a student at a well-resourced school may have access to internships, exchange programmes, leadership positions, or alumni connections that point them towards better opportunities. By contrast, a student at a less privileged school may be just as talented, but may have fewer chances to show what they can do.
components.chinese: 例如，一個在資源充足學校讀書的學生，可能有機會接觸實習、交流計劃、領導崗位，或者透過校友網絡接觸到更好的機會。相比之下，一個在資源較少學校就讀的學生，即使同樣有才華，也可能較少機會去展示自己的能力。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a student at a well-resourced school may have access to
internships, exchange programmes, leadership positions, or alumni connections
that point them towards better opportunities. By contrast, a student at a less
privileged school may be just as talented, but may have fewer chances to show
what they can do.
[例如，一個在資源充足學校讀書的學生，可能有機會接觸實習、交流計劃、領導崗位，或者
透過校友網絡接觸到更好的機會。相比之下，一個在資源較少學校就讀的學生，即使同樣有
才華，也可能較少機會去展示自己的能力。]

components.english: So yes, the type of school can influence career success, because it often acts like a springboard that gives some students a head start.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，學校類型的確可以影響事業成功，因為它往往像一塊跳板，讓某些學生能夠較早起步。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, the type of school can influence career success, because it
often acts like a springboard that gives some students a head start.
[所以，是的，學校類型的確可以影響事業成功，因為它往往像一塊跳板，讓某些學生能夠
較早起步。]

components.english: That said, I would not say school type decides everything, because in the long run, personal qualities still matter enormously.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不會說學校類型決定一切，因為從長遠來看，個人特質仍然非常重要。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I would not say school type decides everything, because in
the long run, personal qualities still matter enormously.
[不過，我也不會說學校類型決定一切，因為從長遠來看，個人特質仍然非常重要。]

components.english: A strong school can open the first door, but it cannot walk the path for you. Career success usually depends on how hard someone works, how well they adapt, and whether they keep learning as life changes around them. Plenty of people come from ordinary schools and still go far, because they are resilient, resourceful, and willing to raise their game when it matters.
components.chinese: 一間好的學校可以替你打開第一道門，但它不能代替你走完之後的路。事業成功通常仍然取決於一個人有多努力、能否靈活適應，以及當周圍環境改變時，他是否願意繼續學習。很多人即使來自很普通的學校，最後仍然可以走得很遠，因為他們有韌性、有方法，也願意在關鍵時刻提升自己。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: A strong school can open the first door, but it cannot walk the
path for you. Career success usually depends on how hard someone works, how
well they adapt, and whether they keep learning as life changes around them.
Plenty of people come from ordinary schools and still go far, because they are
resilient, resourceful, and willing to raise their game when it matters.
[一間好的學校可以替你打開第一道門，但它不能代替你走完之後的路。事業成功通常仍然
取決於一個人有多努力、能否靈活適應，以及當周圍環境改變時，他是否願意繼續學習。很
多人即使來自很普通的學校，最後仍然可以走得很遠，因為他們有韌性、有方法，也願意在
關鍵時刻提升自己。]

components.english: For instance, someone from a less famous school may start with fewer advantages, but if they build strong skills, communicate well, and prove themselves consistently at work, employers are likely to value their performance more than the name of the school on their CV.
components.chinese: 例如，一個來自名氣較小學校的人，起初可能優勢較少；但如果他能建立紮實能力、表達得體，並且在工作中持續證明自己，僱主很可能會更重視他的實際表現，而不是履歷上那間學校的名字。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, someone from a less famous school may start with
fewer advantages, but if they build strong skills, communicate well, and prove
themselves consistently at work, employers are likely to value their performance
more than the name of the school on their CV.
[例如，一個來自名氣較小學校的人，起初可能優勢較少；但如果他能建立紮實能力、表達
得體，並且在工作中持續證明自己，僱主很可能會更重視他的實際表現，而不是履歷上那間
學校的名字。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the type of school you go to can shape your starting point, but it does not write your whole future.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說你就讀的學校類型可以影響你的起跑點，但它並不會把你整個未來都寫死。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the type of school you go to can shape your
starting point, but it does not write your whole future.
[總結來說，我會說你就讀的學校類型可以影響你的起跑點，但它並不會把你整個未來都寫

question.english: What changes do you think will happen in the classroom in the near future?
question.chinese: 你認為在不久的將來，課堂會出現甚麼變化？

source_lines.text: What changes do you think will happen in the classroom in the near future?
你認為在不久的將來，課堂會出現甚麼變化？

components.english: I think classrooms in the near future will become much more technology-driven and flexible, rather than relying so heavily on the traditional chalk-and-talk model.
components.chinese: 我認為，在不久的將來，課堂會變得更加科技化，也更加靈活，而不會再那麼依賴傳統那種單向講授的教學模式。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think classrooms in the near future will become much more
technology-driven and flexible, rather than relying so heavily on the traditional
chalk-and-talk model.
[我認為，在不久的將來，課堂會變得更加科技化，也更加靈活，而不會再那麼依賴傳統那種
單向講授的教學模式。]

components.english: The main reason is that digital tools are now woven into everyday life, so it is only natural that education will move in the same direction. I imagine students will use more tablets, interactive screens, and AI-based learning platforms that can adapt to their pace and needs. In that sense, the classroom may start to feel less like a one-size-fits-all system and more like a space where learning can be tailored to different students. That could make lessons more engaging and, ideally, more effective as well.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，數碼工具現在已經深深融入日常生活，所以教育朝着同一方向發展，其實是很自然的事。我想像未來學生會使用更多平板電腦、互動屏幕，以及能按照個人節奏和需要調整內容的人工智能學習平台。從這個角度來說，課堂可能會慢慢不再像一個「一套方法套所有人」的制度，而更像一個能因應不同學生作出調整的學習空間。這樣一來，課堂不但可能更吸引人，也有機會更有效。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that digital tools are now woven into
everyday life, so it is only natural that education will move in the same
direction. I imagine students will use more tablets, interactive screens, and
AI-based learning platforms that can adapt to their pace and needs. In that
sense, the classroom may start to feel less like a one-size-fits-all system and more
like a space where learning can be tailored to different students. That could
make lessons more engaging and, ideally, more effective as well.
[主要原因是，數碼工具現在已經深深融入日常生活，所以教育朝着同一方向發展，其實是
很自然的事。我想像未來學生會使用更多平板電腦、互動屏幕，以及能按照個人節奏和需要
調整內容的人工智能學習平台。從這個角度來說，課堂可能會慢慢不再像一個「一套方法套
所有人」的制度，而更像一個能因應不同學生作出調整的學習空間。這樣一來，課堂不但可
能更吸引人，也有機會更有效。]

components.english: For example, instead of every student doing exactly the same exercise at the same speed, a teacher might ask them to complete tasks on a learning app that gives instant feedback. A student who is struggling could get extra support straight away, while a stronger student could move ahead instead of being held back.
components.chinese: 例如，未來未必再是每個學生都以同樣速度做完全一樣的練習。老師可能會叫學生在學習應用程式上完成任務，而系統會即時提供回饋。如果某位學生跟不上，他就可以立刻得到額外支援；相反，能力較強的學生也可以繼續往前走，而不必被迫停在原地。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, instead of every student doing exactly the same
exercise at the same speed, a teacher might ask them to complete tasks on a
learning app that gives instant feedback. A student who is struggling could get
extra support straight away, while a stronger student could move ahead instead
of being held back.
[例如，未來未必再是每個學生都以同樣速度做完全一樣的練習。老師可能會叫學生在學習
應用程式上完成任務，而系統會即時提供回饋。如果某位學生跟不上，他就可以立刻得到額
外支援；相反，能力較強的學生也可以繼續往前走，而不必被迫停在原地。]

components.english: So, one major change, in my view, is that classrooms will become smarter, more personalised, and less rigid than they are now.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，其中一個主要變化就是：未來的課堂會變得更智能、更個人化，也不像現在那樣僵化。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major change, in my view, is that classrooms will become
smarter, more personalised, and less rigid than they are now.
[所以，在我看來，其中一個主要變化就是：未來的課堂會變得更智能、更個人化，也不像現
在那樣僵化。]

components.english: On top of that, I think classrooms will probably place more emphasis on discussion, creativity, and real-world skills, rather than just memorising facts for exams.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為未來的課堂很可能會更重視討論、創意，以及真實世界所需要的能力，而不只是為了考試去死記硬背。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think classrooms will probably place more emphasis
on discussion, creativity, and real-world skills, rather than just memorising facts
for exams.
[除此之外，我也認為未來的課堂很可能會更重視討論、創意，以及真實世界所需要的能力，
而不只是為了考試去死記硬背。]

components.english: The reason is that information is now at people’s fingertips, so simply storing facts in your head is no longer enough. What really matters is whether students can think critically, solve problems, work with others, and communicate clearly. Because of that, teachers may increasingly act as guides rather than just lecturers. In other words, the classroom could shift from being a place where students passively take things in to one where they actively test ideas, ask questions, and learn by doing.
components.chinese: 原因在於，現在資訊幾乎唾手可得，所以單靠把資料記在腦裡，其實已經不夠了。真正重要的是，學生是否能夠批判思考、解決問題、與人合作，以及清楚表達自己。正因如此，老師未來可能會愈來愈像引導者，而不只是單純講課的人。換句話說，課堂可能會由一個學生被動接收知識的地方，轉變成一個主動測試想法、提出問題、並透過實踐來學習的空間。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: The reason is that information is now at people’s fingertips, so
simply storing facts in your head is no longer enough. What really matters is
whether students can think critically, solve problems, work with others, and
communicate clearly. Because of that, teachers may increasingly act as guides
rather than just lecturers. In other words, the classroom could shift from being
a place where students passively take things in to one where they actively test
ideas, ask questions, and learn by doing.
[原因在於，現在資訊幾乎唾手可得，所以單靠把資料記在腦裡，其實已經不夠了。真正重要
的是，學生是否能夠批判思考、解決問題、與人合作，以及清楚表達自己。正因如此，老師未
來可能會愈來愈像引導者，而不只是單純講課的人。換句話說，課堂可能會由一個學生被動
接收知識的地方，轉變成一個主動測試想法、提出問題、並透過實踐來學習的空間。]

components.english: For instance, students may spend more time doing group projects, presentations, experiments, or case studies instead of just listening quietly and taking notes for the whole lesson. A classroom like that would feel more alive, because students would not just learn knowledge; they would learn how to use it.
components.chinese: 例如，學生未來可能會花更多時間去做小組項目、口頭報告、實驗，或者個案分析，而不只是整節課安靜地聽老師說話和抄筆記。這樣的課堂會更有生命力，因為學生學到的不只是知識本身，而是如何運用知識。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, students may spend more time doing group projects,
presentations, experiments, or case studies instead of just listening quietly and
taking notes for the whole lesson. A classroom like that would feel more alive,
because students would not just learn knowledge; they would learn how to use
it.
[例如，學生未來可能會花更多時間去做小組項目、口頭報告、實驗，或者個案分析，而不只
是整節課安靜地聽老師說話和抄筆記。這樣的課堂會更有生命力，因為學生學到的不只是
知識本身，而是如何運用知識。]

components.english: Overall, I think classrooms in the near future will become more digital, more interactive, and more student-centred. However, even if the tools change, I do not think the human side of teaching will disappear, because a good teacher still plays a key role in inspiring, guiding, and bringing learning to life.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我認為未來的課堂會變得更數碼化、更互動，也更以學生為中心。不過，即使工具改變了，我也不認為教學中「以人為本」的那一面會消失，因為一位好的老師始終在啟發學生、引導方向，以及讓學習真正活起來這方面扮演關鍵角色。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I think classrooms in the near future will become more
digital, more interactive, and more student-centred. However, even if the tools
change, I do not think the human side of teaching will disappear, because a good
teacher still plays a key role in inspiring, guiding, and bringing learning to life.
[總結來說，我認為未來的課堂會變得更數碼化、更互動，也更以學生為中心。不過，即使工
具改變了，我也不認為教學中「以人為本」的那一面會消失，因為一位好的老師始終在啟發
學生、引導方向，以及讓學習真正活起來這方面扮演關鍵角色。]
